summary: Started decompiling sysdolphin baselib HSD_Shadow by adding src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c and moving the first shadow allocation helpers out of shadow.s. HSD_ShadowGetAllocData and HSD_ShadowInitAllocData were implemented in C, while HSD_ShadowAlloc was documented as a NON_MATCHING C version with an inline-asm fallback for the matching build. The PR also named several related baselib symbols used by shadow allocation and camera setup, including HSD_CObjAlloc, HSD_CObjSetScissorx4, HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4, allocShadowTObj, HSD_ImageDescAlloc, and the bss allocator variable shadow_alloc_data. Build lists were updated so shadow.o is built from src alongside the remaining asm shadow object.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c with C implementations of HSD_ShadowGetAllocData and HSD_ShadowInitAllocData.
Added a C reconstruction of HSD_ShadowAlloc under #ifdef NON_MATCHING and kept an inline-asm HSD_ShadowAlloc fallback for the matching build.
Removed the corresponding HSD_ShadowGetAllocData, HSD_ShadowInitAllocData, and HSD_ShadowAlloc assembly block from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s.
Renamed the shadow allocator bss symbol from lbl_804C25D8 to shadow_alloc_data and updated shadow.s plus include/variables.h references.
Renamed CObj-related assembly globals and callsites: func_8036A290 to HSD_CObjAlloc, func_8036A010 to HSD_CObjSetScissorx4, and func_8036A110 to HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4.
Renamed TObj-related globals and callsites: func_8036118C to allocShadowTObj and func_803612F8 to HSD_ImageDescAlloc.
Added prototypes in include/functions.h for allocShadowTObj, HSD_ImageDescAlloc, HSD_CObjSetScissorx4, HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4, and HSD_CObjAlloc.
Updated e_files.mk and obj_files.mk to include src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.o while retaining asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.o for remaining assembly.
smart_moves: Used a partial decomp strategy: small allocator-access functions were moved to C immediately, while the harder HSD_ShadowAlloc remained matching through inline assembly with a NON_MATCHING C version documenting intended structure.
Preserved build correctness by adding the new C object without removing the remaining asm shadow object, allowing incremental migration of shadow.s.

Named dependencies around the target function first, which made the HSD_ShadowAlloc C reconstruction readable: camera allocation, texture allocation, image-desc allocation, viewport setup, and scissor setup are all expressed with semantic names.
Replaced anonymous lbl_804C25D8 with shadow_alloc_data, making allocator ownership clear and reducing future ambiguity in shadow-related code.
Kept constants and small-data float labels external in shadow.c rather than guessing new named constants, avoiding unsupported symbol invention while still enabling C progress.
decomp_lessons: For baselib allocator modules, first identify the module-specific HSD_ObjAllocData variable and expose GetAllocData/InitAllocData in C; these often match cleanly and anchor later struct work.
When a target function is not yet matching, a NON_MATCHING C version plus matching inline-asm fallback can document field offsets and control flow without regressing the build.
Renaming shared helper functions can unlock readability across unrelated assembly callsites; this PR propagated HSD_CObjAlloc into melee and baselib asm callers, not just shadow.c.
Build-system edits are part of decomp migration: add src object files in obj_files.mk/e_files.mk while leaving the asm object for functions not yet converted.

Allocator size evidence is useful for struct validation: HSD_ShadowInitAllocData calls HSD_ObjAllocInit with sizeof(HSD_Shadow) and alignment/count argument 4, while the original assembly used a 0x28 object size.

Shadow allocation initializes a camera, a TObj, and an image descriptor, then sets 256x256 image dimensions and matching 0..256 viewport/scissor bounds; these relationships are useful context for future shadow matching.
naming_conventions: HSD_Shadow* is used for public shadow module functions: HSD_ShadowGetAllocData, HSD_ShadowInitAllocData, HSD_ShadowAlloc, HSD_ShadowRemove.
HSD_CObj* names are used for public camera object helpers: HSD_CObjAlloc, HSD_CObjSetScissorx4, HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4.
HSD_ImageDescAlloc follows the HSD type prefix plus action convention for image descriptor allocation.
allocShadowTObj is lower camelCase rather than HSD_*; the diff provides no rationale, but the name indicates a shadow-specific texture object allocator.
Module allocator globals can be renamed from raw lbl_* labels to descriptive snake_case such as shadow_alloc_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted matched leaf/simple functions to C first and deleted their assembly from shadow.s.
Used #ifdef NON_MATCHING to keep a readable C implementation of HSD_ShadowAlloc while preserving exact assembly in the #else branch.
Inline assembly in shadow.c updated branch targets to newly named symbols such as HSD_CObjAlloc, allocShadowTObj, HSD_ImageDescAlloc, HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4, and HSD_CObjSetScissorx4.
Assembly callsites in other files were updated to renamed globals without changing instruction bytes, improving symbol information while preserving matching.
The HSD_ShadowAlloc reconstruction records field offsets through struct-style accesses: camera, texture, texture->imagedesc, scaleS/scaleT/transS/transT, and intensity.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR merged very quickly after creation; no explicit reviewer rationale is available from the dump.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match HSD_ShadowAlloc or remove its inline assembly fallback.
Search for subsequent struct definitions or field renames for HSD_Shadow, HSD_TObj, HSD_ImageDesc, and HSD_CObj.
Check whether allocShadowTObj was later renamed to an HSD_* public-style name or kept as shadow-specific internal naming.
Look for PRs that name lbl_804DE768, lbl_804DE76C, lbl_804DE770, and lbl_804DE774 shadow/camera constants.
Search later baselib shadow work for HSD_ShadowRemove and remaining functions still in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s.
